summary.intent: Address PR #555 review comments on the moltis/streamable-http branch, including renaming error variants to be transport-agnostic, fixing stale error messages, adding transport validation, and adding StreamableHttp unit tests.
summary.outcome: All requested changes were implemented and verified: renamed NotSseTransport→NotRemoteTransport and MissingSseUrl→MissingRemoteUrl, fixed stale error messages, added a manual Display impl for TransportType, added StreamableHttp tests, added transport validation in check_semantic_warnings. All 172 config tests and MCP tests passed; changes were committed and pushed.

repo: TransportType enum lives in crates/mcp/src/types.rs and has no Display impl by default — serde rename handles deserialization but not formatting.
MCP server config schema is in crates/config/src/schema.rs; transport is a string field there.
Config semantic validation (check_semantic_warnings) lives in crates/config/src/validate.rs and is tested with 172 tests.
The workspace does not use strum, derive_more, or any enum-display macro crate — Display impls must be written manually.

code.finding: NotSseTransport renamed to NotRemoteTransport and MissingSseUrl renamed to MissingRemoteUrl to be transport-agnostic.
Manual Display impl added for TransportType since no derive macro is available in the workspace.
Transport field validation added inside check_semantic_warnings to catch invalid transport strings at config parse time.
StreamableHttp unit tests added and stale SSE-specific error message updated to use TransportType Display.

workflow: Cargo check before running tests caught the missing Display impl early.
Running targeted test subsets (parse_server_config, mcp, config) is faster than full workspace test for iterative validation.
Manual Display impl (8 lines) preferred over adding a new crate dependency for a single use case, consistent with repo's minimal-dependency philosophy.

friction: TransportType had no Display impl, causing a compile error when format!("{}", transport) was first used — required an extra discovery loop (grep → read registry.rs → add impl).
MissingSseUrl appeared unused in grep results, requiring extra investigation to confirm it was safe to rename.
open_items: If more enum Display needs arise elsewhere, consider evaluating strum or derive_more for the workspace to avoid repeating manual impls.
The McpServerConfig.url comment was stale and was updated, but broader doc/comment audit for SSE-specific language in the codebase was not performed.
